filing_deadline_notes: Disease must develop or injury must occur within 6 months of participating in fire fighting, or training/drill that actually involves fire fighting
notes: Covers active members of municipal fire departments AND volunteer firefighters associations. Firefighter defined broadly to include administrative/other duty members who aid in fire extinguishment. 2-year membership minimum required. Temporal trigger: disease/injury within 6 months of actual firefighting or firefighting training/drill.

filing_deadline_notes: Disease must develop or injury must occur within 6 months of participating in fire fighting, or training/drill that actually involves fire fighting
notes: Volunteer firefighters association members explicitly included in 'firefighter' definition under Sec. 328. Same 2-year membership and 6-month temporal trigger requirements.

filing_deadline_notes: Disease must develop or injury must occur within 6 months of participating in fire fighting, or training/drill that actually involves fire fighting
notes: Same statute as cardiovascular row (Sec. 328). Pulmonary disease covered alongside cardiovascular. Same 2-year minimum and 6-month firefighting-activity trigger.

filing_deadline_notes: Disease must develop or injury must occur within 6 months of participating in fire fighting, or training/drill that actually involves fire fighting
notes: Volunteer firefighters association members explicitly included. Same conditions as career firefighter row.

condition_specific: hepatitis (A, B, C, or any generally recognized strain), meningococcal meningitis, tuberculosis
notes: Covers 'emergency rescue or public safety workers' with high risk of occupational exposure. Firefighter defined as active member of municipal fire department or volunteer fire association. Pre-employment physical required (if employer requires it) — must test negative for hepatitis/TB. Worker must sign affidavit declaring no outside exposure sources, no transfusions, no high-risk sexual behavior, no IV drug use. Preemployment test exemption for workers employed on the effective date of the section. Multi-employer rule: last employer of injurious exposure is liable. Presumption explicitly applies for disability retirement purposes.

condition_specific: hepatitis (A, B, C, or any generally recognized strain), meningococcal meningitis, tuberculosis
notes: Volunteer fire association members explicitly included in firefighter definition under Sec. 328-A. Employer includes entities for which person provides volunteer services. Same affidavit, pre-employment testing, and immunization requirements.

condition_specific: hepatitis (A, B, C, or any generally recognized strain), meningococcal meningitis, tuberculosis
notes: EMS person defined as licensed under Title 32, ch. 2-B, employed by or providing voluntary service to a licensed ambulance service or nontransporting EMS. Must run high risk of occupational exposure. Same affidavit, pre-employment testing, and immunization conditions.

condition_specific: hepatitis (A, B, C, or any generally recognized strain), meningococcal meningitis, tuberculosis
notes: Law enforcement officer as defined in Title 25, sec. 2801-A, sub. 5. Must run high risk of occupational exposure. Same affidavit, pre-employment testing, and immunization conditions.

condition_specific: hepatitis (A, B, C, or any generally recognized strain), meningococcal meningitis, tuberculosis
notes: Corrections officer as defined in Title 25, sec. 2801-A, sub. 2. Must run high risk of occupational exposure through custody/restraint duties. Same affidavit, pre-employment testing, and immunization conditions.

condition_specific: kidney cancer, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, colon cancer, leukemia, brain cancer, bladder cancer, multiple myeloma, prostate cancer, testicular cancer, breast cancer, gynecologic cancer
filing_deadline_notes: Diagnosis must be within 10 years of last active employment as firefighter OR prior to attaining age 70, whichever occurs first
notes: Covers municipal fire department members and volunteer fire association members (via 'employed' definition that includes volunteer members with no compensation other than injury/death benefits). Minimum 5 years employment; must have regularly responded to firefighting or emergency calls (exception for State Fire Marshal investigators/sergeants and forest protection employees). Pre-employment test required (must test negative for cancer). Firefighter must sign affidavit that cancer is not prevalent in blood-related family and no substantial lifetime carcinogen exposure other than through firefighting. State Fire Marshal investigators/sergeants must certify use of protective equipment per departmental policies.

condition_specific: kidney cancer, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, colon cancer, leukemia, brain cancer, bladder cancer, multiple myeloma, prostate cancer, testicular cancer, breast cancer, gynecologic cancer
filing_deadline_notes: Diagnosis must be within 10 years of last active employment as firefighter OR prior to attaining age 70, whichever occurs first
notes: 'Employed' definition in Sec. 328-B explicitly includes volunteer fire association members with no compensation other than injury/death benefits. Same 5-year service, pre-employment test, affidavit, and post-termination window requirements.

Rebuttable by clear and convincing evidence. Diagnosis must be by allopathic or osteopathic physician (Title 32, ch. 48 or 36) with psychiatry specialization, or licensed psychologist (Title 32, ch. 56). Personnel action exclusion: PTSD not compensable if resulting from disciplinary action, work evaluation, job transfer, layoff, demotion, termination, or similar good-faith employer action. Firefighter defined as in Sec. 328-A.
Law enforcement officer as defined in Sec. 328-A (Title 25, sec. 2801-A). Same diagnosis and rebuttability requirements. Personnel action exclusion applies.
Corrections officer as defined in Sec. 328-A (Title 25, sec. 2801-A). Same diagnosis and rebuttability requirements. Personnel action exclusion applies.
Emergency medical services worker as defined in Sec. 328-A. Same diagnosis and rebuttability requirements. Personnel action exclusion applies.
